title: Two Novel Species of Paenibacillus praedii sp. nov. and Ectobacillus soil sp. nov.
author_string: Gao, Peng, Lu, Xie-Feng, Yao, Ling, Xu, Yuan-Jia, Zhang, Shu-Yi et al.
abstract: Two novel aerobic, rod-shaped, motile bacterial strains, designated as strains sgz302251<sup>T</sup> and sgz5001026<sup>T</sup>, were both isolated from paddy soils. 16S rRNA gene sequences analysis revealed that strain sgz302251<sup>T</sup> exhibited 98.3% similarity with its closest reference strain Paenibacillus vietnamensis N4<sup>T</sup>, while strain sgz5001026<sup>T</sup> had the highest similarity of 97.5% to Ectobacillus polymachus KACC 18242<sup>T</sup>. The average nucleotide identity (ANI) and digital DNA-DNA hybridization (dDDH) values between strains sgz302251<sup>T</sup>, sgz5001026<sup>T</sup> and their closest reference strains were 86.0% and 21.2%, 84.9% and 22.9%, respectively. The genomic DNA G + C contents of sgz302251<sup>T</sup> and sgz5001026<sup>T</sup> were 63.4% and 68.8%, respectively. Strain sgz302251<sup>T</sup> grew at 20-37 °C (optimum 30 °C), pH 5.0-9.0 (optimum 6.5-7.5) and tolerated 0-1.0% NaCl (optimum 0.5%, w/v), while strain sgz5001026<sup>T</sup> grew at 4-45 °C (optimum 37 °C), pH 5.0-9.0 (optimum 6.0) and 0-0.5% NaCl (optimum 0%). The quinone in both strains was MK-7 and major polar lipids were diphosphatidylglycerol (DPG), phosphatidylethanolamine (PE) and phosphatidylglycerol (PG). The main fatty acids (> 5%) of strain sgz302251<sup>T</sup> were anteiso-C<sub>15:0</sub>, anteiso-C<sub>17:0</sub>, and C<sub>16:0</sub>, whereas strain sgz5001026<sup>T</sup> included anteiso-C<sub>15:0</sub>, iso-C<sub>15:0</sub>, iso-C<sub>16:0</sub> and iso-C<sub>14:0</sub>. Based on the above evidence, the proposed names of the two novel species are Paenibacillus praedii sp. nov. (type strain sgz302251<sup>T</sup> = MCCC 1K09633<sup>T</sup> = KCTC 43808<sup>T</sup>), Ectobacillus soli sp. nov. (type strain sgz5001026<sup>T</sup> = MCCC 1K09373<sup>T</sup> = KCTC 25903<sup>T</sup>), respectively.
